generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A 100W heating element for thermal regulation of the Physical Twin system, interfaced via Raspberry Pi GPIO"
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical actuator for airflow control in thermal management of the Physical Twin system"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensorIncubator : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor deployed across the incubator to monitor thermal zones with measurement range -55°C to +125°C and ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataIncubator
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataIncubator : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorIncubator
    DTDFVocab:transmissionProtocol GPIO
    DTDFVocab:samplingFrequency PeriodicSampling
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DifferentialEquationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavior model using differential equations for predicting car motion under varying operational conditions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithms, StateEstimation
]

instance CADGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric representation of car components (wheels, chassis) for structural analysis via CAD models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalForceSimulation, FaultDetection
]

instance EnvironmentalForceSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Force-field simulation model representing aerodynamic drag and road conditions acting on the Physical Twin"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DynamicOptimization
]

instance CalibrationParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Refined model parameters derived from sensor data calibration for Cyber-Physical System alignment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataIncubator
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithms
]

instance DynamicBehaviorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational metrics (forces, motion logs) collected from Physical Twin sensors for dynamic analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataIncubator
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DynamicOptimization, StateEstimation
]

instance ModelValidationResults : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulation validation outputs confirming model accuracy under current operating conditions"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DynamicBehaviorData
]

// Additional instances for fixed sampling frequency
instance PeriodicSampling : DTDFVocab:SamplingFrequency [
    base:desc "Periodic sampling of sensor data"
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ message broker facilitating real-time synchronization of sensor data to Digital Twin via message queues"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, DynamicOptimization
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithms : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated algorithms refining model parameters using real-time sensor data for Cyber-Physical System alignment"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicOptimization, StateEstimation
]

instance StateEstimation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithms reconstructing hidden operational states from observed sensor data for predictive modeling"
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetection, DynamicOptimization
]

instance DynamicOptimization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic techniques adjusting PT parameters (chassis design, sensor placements) for efficiency/safety optimization"
]

instance FaultDetection : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data-driven techniques identifying anomalies in system performance for safety violations detection"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, CorrectiveAction
]

instance SupervisoryController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated system triggering corrective actions based on safety/optimality thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:enables CorrectiveAction
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine executing differential equation-based dynamics for predicting PT behavior under hypothetical conditions"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicOptimization
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Database storing sensor logs and model outputs for historical analysis and closed-loop feedback"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time performance validation system employing calibration algorithms to update cyber-physical models with sensor-derived data"
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationInsight, OptimalityDeviationInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic model optimization integrating algorithmic techniques to adjust PT parameters for efficiency/safety under varying conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterAdjustmentAction, PerformanceEnhancementAction
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "System simulating PT behavior under current and hypothetical scenarios using differential equation-based dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:provides BehaviorPredictionInsight, ScenarioAnalysisInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated intervention system triggering corrective actions when safety/optimality thresholds are violated"
    DTDFVocab:provides CorrectiveAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive data representation supporting multimodal visualization (CAD interfaces for structural attributes, dynamic graphs for force-movement analysis)"
    DTDFVocab:provides DiagnosticVisualization, ModelValidationVisualization
]

instance KnowledgeTransferService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cross-disciplinary communication platform consolidating disparate data (sensors, models, algorithms) for unified framework"
    DTDFVocab:provides CrossDisciplinaryInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SafetyViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected deviations from optimal/safe operating conditions identified via state estimators or fault detectors"
]

instance OptimalityDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance metrics showing suboptimal operation conditions requiring optimization adjustments"
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjustment of PT parameters (e.g., chassis design, sensor placements) to maximize efficiency/safety"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceEnhancementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization-based adjustments to improve system performance under varying conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance BehaviorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted PT behavior outcomes under current and hypothetical scenarios via differential equation simulations"
]

instance ScenarioAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of PT behavior under different operational conditions for decision-making support"
]

instance CorrectiveAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated or human-driven adjustments to PT via actuators when safety/optimality thresholds are violated"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DiagnosticVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Multimodal visual representation aiding engineers in diagnosing system issues"
]

instance ModelValidationVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive CAD-like interface showing model alignment with physical attributes for validation"
]

instance CrossDisciplinaryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Unified representation consolidating data from CAD modelers, dynamic analysts, and sensor data for cross-disciplinary communication"
]

// C12: Environment
instance PhysicalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensorIncubator, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DifferentialEquationModel, CADGeometryModel, EnvironmentalForceSimulation,
                 CalibrationParameters, DynamicBehaviorData, ModelValidationResults,
                 RabbitMQBroker, CalibrationAlgorithms, StateEstimation,
                 DynamicOptimization, FaultDetection, SupervisoryController,
                 SimulationEngine, TimeSeriesDatabase
]

// C13: Components
instance CarWheelsComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical components representing wheel sizes and mechanical properties in CAD geometry model"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEnvironment
]

instance CarChassisComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Structural component representing chassis shape and material composition in CAD geometry model"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEnvironment
]

instance ForceMeasurementComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Data collection component for aerodynamic drag and road condition forces acting on the Physical Twin"
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalTwinEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) synchronizes data collection from the Physical Twin (PT) through sensors over a **temporal granularity of 'over time'**, implying periodic or event-driven data acquisition rather than continuous real-time streams. Calibration algorithms periodically update the DT models with the best parameter estimates derived from the collected data, ensuring alignment between the digital and physical representations. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies, the workflow involves a **closed-loop feedback mechanism** where monitors evaluate system performance and trigger interventions (human or algorithmic) upon detected violations, suggesting a **discrete, event-based temporal coordination** rather than continuous synchronization."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system described in the documents follows a **centralized architecture pattern**, where the DT integrates **models, data streams from sensors**, and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators, optimizers) to simulate and optimize the behavior of the Physical Twin (PT). The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models dynamically with real-time data, ensuring alignment with the PT’s operational parameters. The system-wide coordination is achieved through a **closed-loop feedback mechanism**: data from sensors informs monitors, which detect deviations from optimal performance. Violations trigger interventions (human or algorithmic) to adjust the PT. The DT’s structure is depicted as a cohesive unit in the implied figure (Fig. 1), emphasizing **resource management** via centralized control of model updates and monitoring workflows. No distributed orchestration patterns or specific integration tools (e.g., middleware, APIs) are mentioned, but the DT’s core relies on **real-time synchronization** and **algorithm-driven decision-making** for seamless operation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) in the provided context is implicitly defined through two key phases:
1. **Development Phase**:
   - **Representation Types**: Initially, models are created to represent specific aspects of the system, such as static models (e.g., CAD models for physical dimensions like wheel sizes, chassis shapes, or car styles) and dynamic models (e.g., differential equation-based models for dynamics like force-driven movement).
   - **Transition Management**: Once the system is deployed, the static and dynamic models are integrated into the DT to ensure continuous alignment with the Physical Twin (PT). This involves updating models with real-time data from sensors and refining them through calibration algorithms (e.g., non-linear least squares) to match operational conditions.

2. **Operational Phase**:
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: The DT remains active post-deployment, incorporating real-time data, model refinements, and algorithmic interventions (e.g., fault detection, state estimation) to sustain value. There is no explicit mention of formalized lifecycle stages like 'Conceptualization & Design' or 'Integration & Deployment,' but the integration of models into the DT after deployment is emphasized as a continuous process.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validation**:
  The fidelity of DT models is critical for accurate predictions and operational decisions. The provided documents emphasize that models must be **calibrated dynamically** using real-time data from the PT to ensure alignment with physical behavior. Key considerations include:
  - **Validation Methods**: Models are validated through **simulation-to-reality comparisons**, where predictions from the DT are cross-checked against observed PT performance under identical conditions.
  - **Uncertainty Quantification**: While not explicitly quantified in the documents, implicit reliance on **algorithmic calibration** (e.g., via iterative parameter updates) suggests that uncertainty is managed through iterative refinement of model parameters.
  - **Error Detection/Correction**: The DT employs **monitoring systems** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to detect deviations between predicted and actual PT behavior. Violations trigger **automated or human-driven corrections** to models or PT operations.

  **Quality Assurance Processes**:
  The integration of models into the DT framework ensures **long-term maintenance** rather than discard, with continuous validation loops. The documents imply that fidelity is preserved through:
  - **Data-Driven Model Updates**: Sensors provide real-time feedback to adjust models dynamically.
  - **Safety-Critical Validation**: Models must pass rigorous checks to ensure they do not introduce risks (e.g., incorrect predictions leading to unsafe PT states).
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** relies on a **local edge infrastructure** hosted on a **Raspberry Pi** with built-in **Wi-Fi and Bluetooth** capabilities. Communication is mediated by **RabbitMQ**, a lightweight message broker deployed on the same device for low-latency synchronization. The network topology is **single-node** (Raspberry Pi as both host and broker), with no explicit mention of redundancy or failover mechanisms. Bandwidth and latency requirements are implicitly optimized for real-time sensor data streams (e.g., temperature updates) and control commands, leveraging the Pi’s embedded processing. Security protocols are not explicitly detailed, but the RabbitMQ broker’s default security measures (e.g., TLS for encrypted communication) are assumed to protect data integrity during transmission.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s hosting deployment** is implemented as an **on-premise/edge deployment model**, with the entire system (RabbitMQ server, Raspberry Pi, and sensor nodes) co-located on a single device. The computational requirements are met by the **Raspberry Pi’s embedded CPU**, which supports real-time processing of sensor data and control logic. Storage is managed via the Pi’s onboard SSD or external interfaces, though specific capacity requirements are not quantified. Scalability mechanisms are limited to the edge deployment, with no mention of cloud-based expansion. Availability/reliability is ensured through the Raspberry Pi’s hardware redundancy (e.g., dual-core ARM Cortex-A7) and the RabbitMQ broker’s built-in resilience to message loss, though no explicit uptime guarantees are provided.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "A Digital Twin (DT) system incorporates **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment with real-time sensor data to ensure accurate parameter estimation. The DT employs **monitoring systems** that verify whether the system operates safely and optimally by detecting deviations from expected behavior. While the document does not explicitly mention authentication systems or access control mechanisms, it implies a **safe protocol** in the low-level driver to maintain the device in a controlled, fail-safe state. The DT structure relies on algorithms that detect anomalies (e.g., faults, suboptimal performance) and trigger corrective actions (e.g., human intervention or automated adjustments) to mitigate risks. No explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, intrusion detection) or formal risk assessment methodologies is provided, but the integration of monitoring and calibration aligns with safety-critical system considerations."
]
